Recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that:
1.8 The MT cancels the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Accountant with the
original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the
preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed in
Section 59 of Chapter 3 of the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I, to ensure recording
of such cancellation;
1.9 The MA, if possible, coordinates with the bank to determine whether checks
claimed by the payees were not yet presented for payment and secure the
addressees of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their
possession for replacement; and
1.10 The MA exerts effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks and for
those without any check number, disbursement voucher number and date of
issue, should get the necessary information and if per record these have been
stale for many years, consider the reversion of these payables to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

Recommendations:
2.14 We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant:
2.15 To make the necessary adjusting entries in the book of accounts in order to
reflect the correct Cash in Bank balance as shown in the Adjusted Cash in Bank
balance per BRS and to correctly state the balances of the affected accounts
whether real or nominal shown in the FS as of December 31, 2021;
2.16 To ensure that all valid reconciling items be recorded and adjusted in the books
are properly supported with complete documents; and
2.17 To promptly prepare adjusting entries as to items that are clear and evident as
reflected in the bank statements such as interest income and taxes withheld.
OIC-Municipal Treasurer:
2.18 To secure timely the bank statements from its depository accounts and request
list of credit memo and debit memo from the latter ahead of the schedule and
submit copies of the same to the Office of the MA that will facilitate the
preparation of the BRS; and
2.19 To provide and submit to the Office of the MA information substantiated with
complete documents as to items directly affected the cash balance of the
Municipality per bank which are not recorded per book. As to the other
reconciling items where the Office of the MT has no information as to the
transaction that transpired directly in the bank, the former to send
letters/correspondences to the source agency who deposited the amount to
determine the true nature of the transaction and to secure documents from the
same.
2.20 To provide and submit to the Office of the MA information substantiated with
complete documents as to items directly affected the cash balance of the
Municipality per bank which are not recorded per book. As to the other
reconciling items where the Office of the MT has no information as to the
transaction that transpired directly in the bank, the former to send
letters/correspondences to the source agency who deposited the amount to
determine the true nature of the transaction and to secure documents from the
same.
Former Municipal Treasurer
2.21 To ensure that debit advice is issued only on a duly processed and approved
payroll; and
2.22 To process all fund transfers from one bank account to another by securing first
that the prepared payroll was duly approved by the proper official concerned.
Sangguniang Bayan Member:
2.23 Avoid signing debit advice for a consolidated payment of payroll without first
approving the entire amount of the total payroll reflected in the debit advice to
prevent transferring funds without supporting documents that already bear the
approval of the proper official concerned; and
2.24 Way forward, exercise cautious and proper diligence before affixing signatures
in approving documents.

Recommendations:
3.15 We reiterated that the management:
3.16 Require the MGSO and MA to observe the procedures outlined in Section
7 of the Circular in order to exhaust available means to locate missing PPEs.
3.17 Effect the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the disposal of the obsolete
and unserviceable properties
3.18 Identify the Semi-expendable items/equipment and properties turned-over
to the barangays and other entities to facilitate proper derecognition and transfer
of accounts.
3.19 Require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to segregate the components of the
Road Networks, namely, (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope
protection structures, and (d) other miscellaneous structures, as the case may
be, guided by the cited provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008, so that the MA
can accurately reflect the annual Depreciation Expense, Accumulated
Depreciation, and the Net Book Value of the account. Moreover, provide
depreciation for the aforementioned infrastructure assets in the ensuing year and
adjust accordingly the corresponding accumulated depreciation in the
government equity

Recommendations:
4.11 We recommended the management to:
4.12 Require the ME and MA to review and evaluate the cost incurred of all
physically completed projects that are still recorded under the CIP accounts to
facilitate recording/transfer of its cost to the appropriate account classification.
4.13 Further require the ME to submit for inspection all completed infrastructure
projects whether undertaken by contract or administration duly supported with
certified copies of the following documents:
For project by Admin
• Certificate of Final Inspection, Completion, Acceptance & Turn-over
• Statement of Work Accomplished

Recommendations:
5.7 We reiterated that management direct the MA and MT to verify fund sources and
return the balances thereof to afford optimum utilization of government funds.

Recommendation:
6.6 We recommended that the MM and HRMO prioritize the filing-up of the MDRRM
Officer and staff following the qualification standards, position titles and salary
grades set by the IRR of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-CSC Joint Memorandum
Circular No.2014-1 as these are mandatory positions to provide adequate support
to the various MDRRM activities.

Recommendations
7.5 We recommended that the:
A. MM along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the
provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning
the programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and their
implementation. See to it that those development projects identified in the AIP
were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents; and
B. Executive Committee of the Local Development Council identify and prioritize
the development projects that could be implemented during the year by taking
into consideration the technical manpower of the Municipality. Consider
changing the PPAs in the AIPs which cannot be implemented during the year to
other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the
Sangguniang Bayan.
C. Consider the creation of a unit under the Office of the MM to purposely monitor
the various identified PPAs to be implemented during the year funded by the
20% DF, LDRRMF and other funds coming from local and national agencies.

Recommendations:
8.10 To strengthen the risk management capabilities of the Municipal
Government and enable it to manage its vulnerabilities in the event of disruptive
events, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) institutionalize the
PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities:
a. to conduct risk assessment;
b. to identify critical processes and functions;
c. to determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
d. to conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. to formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical processes and
functions and its related steps to be followed to eliminate, if not mitigate, the
impact of the determined disruptions following the prescribed template and be
guided by the PSCP guidebook; and
f. to submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the LCE for approval, and to the
Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.
8.11 We also recommended Management to improve their monitoring of
operational requirements like the submission of PSCP.

Recommendation:
9.8 We recommended that the LCE direct the LPDO to establish GAD database
containing sex disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the
conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender
responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

Recommendation:
10.6 We recommended that the MM to direct the MA and Municipal Legal Officer
(MLO) to make follow-ups by sending Collection Letter to the Supplier in order to
collect the amount of P1.582 million as soon as possible. If the supplier
continuously disregard the LGU’s effort to collect, the LGU to enforce other legal
remedies available which includes the filing of appropriate charges.

27.1 We reiterated that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines in the
granting, utilization and accounting of government funds in the release of financial
assistance to NGOs/POs as embodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October
25, 2007 to ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.
a. It was also recommended that unreliable account balances due to
unsupported/undocumented dormant accounts which have long been
outstanding be written-off following the guidelines and procedures provided
in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 so as to arrive at
a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that management effect the transfer of its public infrastructure and accounts to the books of accounts of the LGU pursuant to the PPSAS and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.
Also the Local Chief Executive to direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
a. Municipal Accountant
1.Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
2.Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;

4.Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
b. Municipal General Services Officer
1.Exert more efforts to locate the remaining public infra that remained not included in the inventory.

3.Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
c. Municipal Engineer
1.Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Return to DAR-ARCP II any unused project funds.

We recommend that management require the Local School Board that the Annual Budget preparation is confined to program, project and activities chargeable to SEF as those expressly enumerated in Section 4.0 of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1. Moreover, the hiring of school board teachers should be based on determined shortages per teacher-deployment analysis of elementary and secondary schools. These teachers should therefore be included in the Registry of Qualified Applicants released by DepEd every year.

The Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the revision and updating of the Local Revenue Code and the proposed Market Code to be able to adjust and/or update the taxes and fees rates pursuant to sections 129 and 191 fo RA 7160 to meet the financial needs of the LGU.
Improve the LGU’s policies and procedures to streamline the operations the local economic enterprises and strengthen collection efforts over spaces rentals to generate more revenues and to create a fair and equal footing between tenants, such as but not limited to:
Require the execution of Lease Contract/Agreement that spells out the basic terms and conditions of the lease.
Requiring the posting security deposits to answer the tenant’s delinquency.
Imposing penalties and sanctions in case of non-payment of rentals and other fees,
Etc.
The Accounting Office
To maintain special accounts consisting of separate sets of ledgers for each of the economic enterprises
Prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended the submission of the Purchase Orders, Contracts, or Delivery Receipts of the above-mentioned transactions to establish the perfection of contracts and establish the validity of the above claims.

We recommended that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of government funds in the release of financial assistance to NGOs/POs asembodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.

It was also recommended that unreliabile account balances due to unsupported/undocumented dormant accounts which have long been outstanding be written-off following the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 so as to arrive at a fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the officials concerned for justification on the charging of said expenses to LDRRMF and if the justification is not tenable, the amount of ₱1,756,810.00 may be disallowed in audit for being irregular.

We recommended that disbursements for food, meals and snacks be adequately supported with the required supporting documents such as delivery receipt, resolution/notices of meeting or its equivalent and proof of distribution/attendance sheet to establish the validity of the claims. Management should strengthen its internal control over transactions involving payment of foods, meals and snacks during meetings and other official activities sponsored by the Municipal Government to prevent any unnecessary and/or excessive expenditures and strictly observe the provisions of Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Further, we recommended that all attendance sheets attached to the claim should properly indicate the name or nature, date and venue of the official activity conducted and bear the names and signatures of the attendees; avoid using attendance sheets with erasures of the date, name of activity and venue as well as designation/office of the attendees as this might cause doubt on the reliability of the attendance sheets and the aforementioned activities.

We recommended that the accountable officers, inventory committee and accounting unit closely coordinate to complete the reconciliation report to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.
Moreover, we recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a more detailed RPCPPE providing the required data, such as property number and unit value for easier monitoring and reconciliation.
Lastly, it was recommended that the Inventory Committee and Accounting Unit continuously conduct the physical count of inventory items and property, plant and equipment, as well as preparing the RPCI and RPCPPE, respectively.
Reconciliation of the Inventory Team’s records with that of the accounting department should also be made to address discrepancies that may arise in the regular course of the agency’s operations, as well as in accordance with Section C.3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.
observations.reason: Reconciliation Report has not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor for review and verification.

observations.reason: Several dormant CIP are still in books of accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue internal guidelines for the proper implementation of the Travel Law and to limit the grant of actual travel expenses to Sangguniang Bayan Members of the Municipality. Should actual expenses be allowed under meritorious cases, the same should be stated in the guidelines and travel authority. Authority to travel shall specify the kind of hotel/lodging/pension house to be taken and the cost per meal, which in all cases be most advantageous to the government from the standpoint of economy and efficiency.
Likewise, the certification by the Municipal Mayor should indicate that the official is entitled to actual travel expenses as well as the justification(s) for such grant.
Further, it was recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant intensify her efforts in ensuring that the rules and regulations of the travel law are consistently applied in the pre-audit of travel and training claims whether the claimant is an official or a regular employee of the LGU to avoid wastage of government funds.
observations.reason: Several disbursement vouchers revealed that SB Members were still allowed to claim actual travel expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU adopt the design standards/criteria and specifications prescribed under DPWH Department Orders on the implementation of infrastructure projects, for uniformity and cost effectiveness of its locally funded infrastructure projects.
We recommended that the original mode of procurement should be properly complied with and any change thereof should be supported with the approval of the Head of the Procuring Entity provided it is in compliance with the conditions thereto. Likewise, the agency also consider partial bid as provided under 32.2.1 of IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: The design standards/criteria and specifications prescribed under DPWH Department orders were still not adopted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management effect the transfer of its public infrastructure accounts to the books of accounts of the LGU pursuant to the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Engineer, MPDO and MBO judiciously plan and prepare the Annual Investment Program. The schedule of implementation should be properly indicated to serve as basis for which the project will be started and the timeline for which it has to be completed. Also, the ICO-Municipal Engineer and MPDO see to it that the execution of these identified projects under the 20% development fund is in accordance with the given timeline embodied in the AIP and the proper monitoring of the same.
We also recommended that Management should consider the implementation of projects by contract so as to avoid delay in the execution of the projects of the LGU for the attainment of the objectives in uplifting and improving the living conditions of their constituents.

We recommended that management remit the remaining amount or balance to BIR within the prescribed period and submit to COA proof of remittances.

We recommended that the cash advances shall be liquidated in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. The concerned officials and employees with the unliquidated cash advances shall effect immediate settlement of the same, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of the COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be enforced.
For dormant receivables, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant request authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit following the guidelines under Section III-A-10 of COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.reason: Several prior years’ unliquidated cash advances are still outstanding as of December 31,2016, notwithstanding demands sent by the OIC-Municipal Accountant for the liquidation/settlement thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

Supervise her revenue collectors particularly delinquent ones so that there will be regular turn-over of collections to the liquidating officer to deter possible misuse or theft of government funds and hasten the recognition of income;

Be the signatory and be the one to sign on the verification and acknowledgement portion of the liquidating officer’s RCDs if such is the case; and

Inform the Municipal Accountant in advance for the amounts to be included in their proper reporting period due to delay in remittance.

It was also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant communicate with the Municipal Treasurer and observe proper cut-off for recognition of income to achieve faithful representation of the amounts stated in the financial statements and in order for the municipality to obtain the right to early disposition of its income to support its operations.

We recommended that the accountant should check and verify the completeness of supporting documents attached to the voucher.
Management should provide specific duties and responsibilities of casuals, job orders and contractual employees.

Accomplishment reports should likewise be submitted by concerned employees before payment of salaries is made.

We recommended that all projects funded under the 20% Development Fund must be specific and the practice of appropriating Priority Identification Fund (PIF) be discontinued considering that the implementation of infrastructure projects is not among the functions of the Sangguniang Bayan under Section 458 of R.A. 7160.
observations.reason: 20% Development Fund still includes lump-sum appropriations for the rehabilitation and maintenance of municipal and barangay roads; however, PIF was already discontinued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU-GFPS should give emphasis on the accuracy and veracity of its Accomplishment Reports through constant communication and reconciliation with the budget officer to achieve reliability of report since this will be submitted to PCW.
We also recommended that the LCE revisit the guidelines set in Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM and consequently adhere to the prescribed structure of the GAD Focal Point System.
Finally, we recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan spearheaded by the SB Chair on the Committee on Women, Children and Family assisted by the LGU GFPS formulate the GAD Code to guide themselves in formulating policies, plans and programs that effectively address gender-related issues.

We recommended that the LGU management adopt an internal control for the effective and cost efficient formulation of estimated costs as basis for the Approved Budget for the Contract of goods to be acquired based on prevailing market prices to attain bid prices from suppliers most favorable to the agency.

Stop the practice of granting overtime pay to the department heads in compliance with DBM Circular No. 10 dated March 29, 1996. Except for the entitlement to overtime pay, department heads, however, are not precluded from rendering overtime services whenever the interest of the service requires.
